channel.title: HR & Careers on jamessmithies.org
channel.description: Recent content in HR & Careers on jamessmithies.org

item.title: HASS Research Software Engineering (RSE) in the UK
item.description: Slides of my talk 'HASS Research Software Engineering (RSE) in the UK', a contribution to the 'Bridging the Digital Divide in HASS Panel' held at the University of Melbourne, August 23, 2023.

item.title: The Phenomenology of Technical Knowledge: Research Software Engineering, Product Development, and Digital Humanities
item.description: Slides of my talk 'The Phenomenology of Technical Knowledge: Research Software Engineering, Product Development, and Digital Humanities' presented via Zoom at the Bridges (Bridges between disciplines: Gender in STEM and Social Sciences) Conference, September 16th 2022.

item.title: Announcing my departure from KDL: New starts, new possibilities
item.description:
Cross-posted from https://kdl.kcl.ac.uk/blog/smithies-departure/.

I’ll be leaving King’s Digital Lab (KDL) this month, after 6 wonderful years, for a new role as Professor of Digital Humanities in King’s Department of Digital Humanities (DDH). Although merely a move over to the other half of Digital Humanities at King’s, and a return to a department I used to hold a fractional appointment in, it represents a significant milestone in my career. I’m grateful to the many people who have supported me to this point, in New Zealand, Australia, Jordan, and the United States, as well as the UK.

item.title: King’s Digital Lab as Experiment and Lifecycle
item.description: This SORSE talk, delivered with KDL Deputy Director Arianna Ciula, describes King’s Digital Lab (KDL), a Research Software Engineering lab operating within the Faculty of Arts and Humanities at King’s College London (UK).  The KDL team of 18 project managers, analysts, designers, engineers, and systems managers specialise in arts & humanities, cultural heritage, and creative industries research and development. The talk provides a current state overview of the lab, and describes our RSE HR roles (see https://zenodo.org/record/2564790) and a relatively recent trial initiative that defines the different ways the team can contribute to research. A more technically-oriented overview of our ongoing work in refining the fundamental components of KDL Software Development Lifecycle (see https://github.com/kingsdigitallab/sdlc-for-rse/) is also provided, including descriptions of our modelling workflows, processes, and efforts to increase sustainability, reproducibility, and research data management best practice (https://zenodo.org/record/3361580#.X09x6mdKhhE).

item.title: King’s Digital Lab Research Software Engineering Career Paths
item.description: The slides of my talk 'King’s Digital Lab Research Software Engineering Career Paths', presented at the Russell Universities Group IT Directors Forum (RUGIT), 19 September 2019. See also Smithies, James. ‘Research Software (RS) Careers: Generic Learnings from King’s Digital Lab, King’s College London’. Zenodo, 7 February 2019. https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.2564790.

item.title: Research Software Engineering in Jordan: The MaDiH (مديح) Project
item.description: Slides of 'Research Software Engineering in Jordan: The MaDiH (مديح) Project', delivered at RSE2019, 18 September, 2019.

item.title: Innovation at the Intersection: Using STS to enable University – Technology Sector Collaboration
item.description: Slides of my talk 'Innovation at the Intersection: Using STS to enable University – Technology Sector Collaboration', delivered at the 4S Society for the Social Studies of Science conference, New Orleans, 04 September 2019.

item.title: Research Software Careers: Establishing Local, National & International Pathways
item.description: Slides for my talk 'Research Software Careers: Establishing Local, National & International Pathways', at the launch of Research Software London, Imperial College London, October 18, 2018.

item.title: From Lab to University: Towards an Institutional RSE Career Pathway
item.description: Slides for my talk 'From Lab to University: Towards an Institutional RSE Career Pathway', delivered at Research Software Engineering 2018, Birmingham University, 03 September 2018.
